response.justification: The role centers on hands-on, high-performance C++/STL development for industrial automation with secure engineering (IEC 62443) and protocol experience (MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA). This candidate is a strong, experienced generalist with notable low-latency and distributed-systems work, but their C++ exposure is minimal and dated (a ~6-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a university thesis), with the bulk of their career in Node.js/TypeScript and DLang. There is no evidence of industrial cybersecurity standards, message-broker C++ systems, or the listed automation protocols. The core technical requirement is largely unmet, so despite solid overall engineering maturity this is a poor fit for the C++ specialty.
strengths: Deep low-latency, high-throughput distributed-systems experience (millions of requests/sec, async I/O, caching)
Strong software-craftsmanship discipline: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, pairing
Broad full-stack and systems range with 13+ years and a CS degree
concerns: Minimal and dated C++/STL experience; no recent hands-on C++ work
No industrial cybersecurity / IEC 62443 / SDL background
No experience with required protocols (MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA) or industrial automation domain
